title: Selliguea conjuncta
taxonomy: Selliguea conjuncta belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. It is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Selliguea conjuncta is also known as the Asian bird's nest fern, due to its unique growth habit and fronds resembling a bird's nest. This species is commonly found growing on trees and rocks in humid environments. Selliguea conjuncta is often cultivated as an ornamental plant for its attractive fronds and ease of care.
uses: Selliguea conjuncta, also known as the fern tree, is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant is reportedly used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some regions, the leaves of Selliguea conjuncta are used as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds. Its medicinal properties may be attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Selliguea conjuncta for medicinal purposes.

intro: Selliguea conjuncta, also known as Thelypteris conjuncta, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
